case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for atomic hydrogen on the provided surface.

analysis_json.message: Most stable config adsorption energy: -2.9616 eV. Bonded Surface Atoms: Cu-1.684Å, Cu-1.774Å, Cu-1.777Å. Is Bound: True. Reactivity Change Detected: False (Bond Changes: 0). plan.reasoning: SMILES [H] is atomic hydrogen (radical with unpaired electron), so adsorbate_type=ReactiveSpecies. No history exists, therefore this is the initial plan. On (111) surfaces atomic H strongly prefers 3-fold hollow sites; we start with the Cu-Cu-Cu hollow as the most common high-symmetry environment on a Cu-rich alloy surface. This satisfies all site-alignment rules (site_type=hollow, one adsorbate index).
